other_observers: Initially with John Vanderpoel. In the afternoon, also by Joey Kellner, Dick Schottler and Randy Lentz.; Yes
specific_location: Along the inlet canal which is at the SW corner of Prewitt Reservoir.
description: About the same size as the Yellow-rumped and Townsend's Warblers it was first seen with. The upperparts were uniformly gray, including the tail, and the bird did not have wingbars. There was a distinct and clear white eye ring, and a little yellow between the dark eye and bill. The chin, breast and underparts were completely yellow, except for a not-very-bright, but distinct, necklace of short gray streaks. This is what makes me think it was an adult female, although a first-year male is quite difficult to eliminate as a possibility. The under tail coverts were bright white, and the legs were quite light and not dark.Behaviors: Seen feeding trees with other warblers, Gnatcatchers, etc, both fairly low and at mid-levels, not in the tree tops. A few times seen trying to catch insects, but unsuccessful.Call: None heardPlumage: Fall adult
